LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SALLY SWANSON ARCHITECTS'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 1, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE THE MOVING AND OPPOSING PARTIES THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE PROOF OF SERVICE FOR THE MOVING PAPERS FILED JUNE 24, 2013, ALONG WITH COURTESY COPIES OF THE REPLY PAPERS FILED JULY 17, 2013. THE OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS FILED JUNE 15, 2013. THE COURTESY COPIES SHOULD BE DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JULY 24, 2013 AT 4:00 PM. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF MOVING PARTY'S MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SALLY SWANSON ARCHITECTS NTC OF MTN AND MTN TO VACATE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE COURT EXERCISES ITS EQUITABLE POWER TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT ON THE GROUND OF EXTRINSIC FRAUD OR MISTAKE. OLIVERA V. GRACE (1942) 19 CAL. 2D 570, 576. DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN A MERITORIOUS DEFENSE (THAT PLAINTIFF'S ASSIGNOR MONROE PERSONNEL SERVICES FAILED TO PROVIDE SERVICES UNDER THE AGREEMENT WITH DEFENDANT SALLY SWANSON ARCHITECTS AND/OR THAT THE AMOUNT OF THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS TWICE WHAT HAD ALLEGEDLY BEEN AGREED TO AND UNSUPPORTED FACTUALLY), A SATISFACTORY EXCUSE (DEFENDANT'S ATTORNEY'S FAILURE TO FILE AN ANSWER AND FAILURE TO NOTIFY DEFENDANT OF NOTICES OF DEFAULT), AND DILIGENCE IN SEEKING TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT ONCE IT WAS DISCOVERED. UPON LEARNING OF THE DEFAULT, DEFENDANT PROMPTLY SOUGHT EX PARTE RELIEF AND THEN FILED THIS MOTION ON JUNE 21, 2013. DEFENDANT SHALL FILE THE PROPOSED ANSWER TO THE COMPLAINT WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER CSR 4761.

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. rULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SALLY SWANSON ARCHITECTS Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Default And Default Judgment is Granted. The Court exercises its equitable power to set aside the default judgment on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Olivera v. Grace (1942) 19 Cal. 2d 570, 576. Defendant has shown a meritorious defense (that Plaintiff's assignor Monroe Personnel Services failed to provide services under the agreement with Defendant Sally Swanson Architects and/or that the amount of the default judgment is twice what had allegedly been agreed to and unsupported factually), a satisfactory excuse (defendant's attorney's failure to file an answer and failure to notify defendant of notices of default), and diligence in seeking to set aside the default once it was discovered. Upon learning of the default, defendant promptly sought ex parte relief and then filed this motion on June 21, 2013. Defendant shall file the proposed Answer to the Complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Susan Crozier CSR 4761.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-24-2013 Continued To Aug-01-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SALLY SWANSON ARCHITECTS's Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment is Continued to August 1, 2013 to give the moving and opposing parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the proof of service for the moving papers filed June 24, 2013, along with courtesy copies of the reply papers filed July 17, 2013. The opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers filed June 15, 2013. The courtesy copies should be delivered to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 24, 2013 at 4:00 pm. The court has courtesy copies of moving party's memorandum of points and authorities. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
